'ساجند' is a verbal/poetic form with the 3rd-person plural ending -ند; here it indicates their position (literally 'they are upon the saj') — in context saj names a raised seat/platform (the word is poetic and the form is preserved for meter). The conjunction 'و' links this to the adjective phrase 'همرنگ عاج' meaning 'of the same color as ivory' (همرنگ = same-colored; عاج = ivory).
segments.s610-g190: 'سزاوار' is an adjective meaning 'deserving/fit for.' It combines with the predicate 'تخت‌اند' (they are of/the throne / they belong on the throne — the −اند ending marks 3rd-person plural). The conjunction 'و' links the second predicate phrase 'زیبای تاج' (literally 'beauty of crown' or 'beautiful for the crown'), where 'زیبای' describes suitability/beauty in relation to 'تاج' (crown).
segments.s610-g191: 'پس‌انگاه' is an adverb meaning 'afterwards/then.' 'گفتش' is the verb 'said' with the clitic -ش ('to him' / 'to her') built in; the following prepositional phrase 'به بهرام پیر' names the addressee explicitly ('to Bahram Pir' — 'پیر' is an epithet meaning 'old' or 'venerable'). The combination is narrative: 'then he said to Bahram Pir.'
segments.s610-g192: که‌ای here introduces direct speech and functions like a vocative 'که ای / که‌ای' = 'O...'; 'شاه' is the vocative 'king.' 'دشمن‌کش' and 'شیرگیر' are compound epithets (enemy-slayer; lion-catcher) applied to the king, linked by 'و.' This is a conventional heroic address.
segments.s610-g81: چنین گفت is adverb + past verb: چنین (thus) modifies گفت (said), a past third-person form. کای is a vocative particle (archaic/poetic) used to address a person directly; it precedes the title شاه. خورشیدچهر is a compound epithet attached to شاه (‘sun‑faced king’).
segments.s610-g82: به کام تو is a prepositional phrase (به = to/for + کام = desire + تو = your) meaning 'to/for your wish'. گرداد is an archaic/poetic verbal form (here optative/dated past meaning 'may it turn / be turned'); it governs the following noun phrase. گردان سپهر is a compound noun phrase where گردان ('revolving') qualifies سپهر ('sky, heaven'). Word order is PV (prepositional phrase) + verb + nominal predicate.
segments.s610-g83: نیارمت is a poetic negative/necessity phrase meaning 'I do not need / it is not necessary for me' (negation + desire/necessity). گفتن is the infinitival/content verb 'to say' and that + clause follows (که introduces the content clause). ایدر بایست = 'here must stand' where ایدر (اینجا) = here and بایست is an older modal/lexical verb meaning 'must/ought' or 'should stand' (second/third‑person sense).
segments.s610-g84: بدین مرز من is a locative/goal phrase: ب (prepositional prefix 'to/in') + دین (here representing 'this' as part of بدین) + مرز ('frontier/border') + من ('of mine' placed after the noun). با سواری دویست = 'with two hundred horsemen' where با = with and سواری (horseman(s)) takes the numeral دویست (two hundred); classical Persian often places the possessor/number after the noun.
segments.s610-g85: سر و نام برزین is a coordinated NP 'head and name of Barzin' where the possessor برزین follows the coordinated nouns (typical Persian genitive/order). برآید is an older/polite present/future or optative third‑person form ('will/may rise' or 'rises'). به ماه is a prepositional phrase 'to the moon' expressing exaltation or comparison.
segments.s610-g86: اگر introduces a conditional clause ('if'). شاد گردَد is a present/subjunctive construction meaning 'becomes glad' or 'is made joyful' (گردد is the common literary present subjunctive/optative of شدن). بدین باغ شاه = 'in this garden, O king' where بدین = ب + 'دین' (here/this) + باغ (garden) and شاه is a vocative address attached at the end.
segments.s610-g87: به برزین is the prepositional object 'to Barzin' (به = to/for + برزین name). چنین گفت is the adverb + verb 'thus said' and شاه جهان ('king of the world') is the subject/title placed after the verb for poetic word order (verb before subject is common in classical poetry).
segments.s610-g88: که introduces reported content ('that'). امروز is a temporal adverb 'today'. طغری شد is a short clause where طغری (tughra, the royal monogram/seal) is the theme and شد (became/happened) serves as the verbal predicate: 'the tughra became (was) ...'. از من نهان = 'hidden from me' (از marking the source/agentive relation, نهان = hidden). The whole phrase means 'that today the royal tughra was hidden from me.'
